nodalMinistryName.label: Ministry Of Petroleum and Natural Gas

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: **The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 (PMUY)** was launched in May 2016 by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to provide free LPG connections to women from poor households. Its main objective is to promote clean cooking fuel like LPG among rural and deprived families who traditionally rely on firewood, coal, or cow-dung cakes. This shift helps reduce health risks for women and minimizes environmental pollution. Under PMUY 2.0, the scheme was further expanded to reach more beneficiaries across India. - The target under the scheme was to release 8 Crore LPG Connections to the deprived households by March 2020. - On 7th September 2019, the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India handed over the 8th Crore LPG connection in Aurangabad, Maharashtra. - The release of 8 Crore LPG connections under the scheme has also helped in increasing the LPG coverage from 62% on 1st May 2016 to 99.8% on 1st April 2021. - Ujjwala 2.0: Additional allocation of 1.6 Crore LPG Connections under PMUY Scheme with special facility to migrant households. The scheme was launched in Mahoba, Uttar Pradesh by the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India, Shri. Narendra Modi.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Cash assistance for PMUY connections is provided by the Government of India - ₹1600 (for a connection 14.2kg cylinder/ ₹1150 for a 5 kg cylinder). The cash assistance covers: 1. Security Deposit of Cylinder – ₹1250 for 14.2 kg cylinder/ ₹ 800 for 5 kg cylinder. 1. Pressure Regulator – ₹150. 1. LPG Hose – ₹100. 1. Domestic Gas Consumer Card – ₹25. 1. Inspection/ Installation/ Demonstration charges – ₹75. Additionally, All PMUY beneficiaries will be provided with first LPG refill and Stove (hotplate) both free of cost along with their deposit-free connection by the Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs).
schemeContent.briefDescription: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MOPNG), introduced the ‘"Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana 2.0" in India. The Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana (PMUY) is a scheme launched by the Government of India in 2016 to provide free LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) connections to women from below-poverty-line.

children.text: The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 (PMUY)

was launched in May 2016 by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to provide free LPG connections to women from poor households. Its main objective is to promote clean cooking fuel like LPG among rural and deprived families who traditionally rely on firewood, coal, or cow-dung cakes. This shift helps reduce health risks for women and minimizes environmental pollution. Under PMUY 2.0, the scheme was further expanded to reach more beneficiaries across India.
The target under the scheme was to release 8 Crore LPG Connections to the deprived households by March 2020.
On 7th September 2019, the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India handed over the 8th Crore LPG connection in Aurangabad, Maharashtra.
The release of 8 Crore LPG connections under the scheme has also helped in increasing the LPG coverage from 62% on 1st May 2016 to 99.8% on 1st April 2021.

Ujjwala 2.0: Additional allocation of 1.6 Crore LPG Connections under PMUY Scheme with special facility to migrant households. The scheme was launched in Mahoba, Uttar Pradesh by the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India, Shri. Narendra Modi.
Cash assistance for PMUY connections is provided by the Government of India - ₹1600 (for a connection 14.2kg cylinder/ ₹1150 for a 5 kg cylinder). The cash assistance covers:
Security Deposit of Cylinder – ₹1250 for 14.2 kg cylinder/ ₹ 800 for 5 kg cylinder.
Pressure Regulator – ₹150.
LPG Hose – ₹100.
Domestic Gas Consumer Card – ₹25.
Inspection/ Installation/ Demonstration charges – ₹75.
Additionally, All PMUY beneficiaries will be provided with first LPG refill and Stove (hotplate) both free of cost along with their deposit-free connection by the Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs).
Step 1: You can download Forms from the official website (given below) or get them from the nearest LPG distributor.
KYC Form
Supplementary KYC Document & Undertaking
ANNEXURE - I
Self-declaration for Family Composition and Address for release  of new connection under PMUY (for Migrant only)
ANNEXURE - II
Mechanics Pre-Installation check of Customers Premises for LPG connection
Step 2: Fill out the application form with the required information, including personal details, bank account information, and LPG distributor preference.
Step 3: Attach the necessary documents along with the application form. The documents typically include proof of identity, proof of address, and proof of BPL category.
Step 4: Submit the completed application form along with the supporting documents to the nearest LPG distributor.

applicationProcess.process_md:
Step 1: You can download Forms from the official website (given below) or get them from the nearest LPG distributor.

- [Supplementary KYC Document & Undertaking](https://www.pmuy.gov.in/documents/Supplementary%20KYC.pdf)- ANNEXURE - I [Self-declaration for Family Composition and Address for release  of new connection under PMUY (for Migrant only)](https://www.pmuy.gov.in/documents/Annexure%20I.pdf)- ANNEXURE - II [Mechanics Pre-Installation check of Customers Premises for LPG connection](https://www.pmuy.gov.in/documents/Annexure%20II.pdf)
Step 2: Fill out the application form with the required information, including personal details, bank account information, and LPG distributor preference.
Step 3: Attach the necessary documents along with the application form. The documents typically include proof of identity, proof of address, and proof of BPL category.
Step 4: Submit the completed application form along with the supporting documents to the nearest LPG distributor.

children.text: Step 1: Apply online for Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 from the
official website
Step 2: Select the option Apply for the New Ujjwala 2.0 Connection.
Step 3: Scroll down to and click on Online Portal. and Select the LPG distribution company name example Indane / Bharatgas / HP Gas.
Step 4: Click on Register Now on the selected LPG distribution company.
Step 5: Select a type of Connection like
Ujjwala 2.0 New Connection.
Step 6: Choose the State, District, Pin Code, and Name of the  Distributor. Then Enter your Mobile Number, Captcha, and OTP.
Step 7: Select the type of KYC New KYC and Normal KYC.
Step 8: Select Migrant Family status in YES or NO
Step 9: For the family Identifier in NO fill in Annexure 1
Step 10: For  family Identifier in Yes fill in Ration Card
Step 11: Fill in all Family Details, Personal details, address details, bank details, Choose Cylinder Types, Select rural or urban and select the declaration and submit.
Step 12: Generate the Reference number and Print your applied form attach all the documents you submitted online and visit the selected Gas agency.

applicationProcess.process_md:
Step 1: Apply online for Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 from the [official website](https://www.pmuy.gov.in/index.aspx).
Step 2: Select the option Apply for the New Ujjwala 2.0 Connection.
Step 3: Scroll down to and click on Online Portal. and Select the LPG distribution company name example Indane / Bharatgas / HP Gas.
Step 4: Click on Register Now on the selected LPG distribution company.
Step 5: Select a type of Connection like **Ujjwala 2.0 New Connection.**
Step 6: Choose the State, District, Pin Code, and Name of the  Distributor. Then Enter your Mobile Number, Captcha, and OTP. 
Step 7: Select the type of KYC New KYC and Normal KYC.
Step 8: Select Migrant Family status in YES or NO
Step 9: For the family Identifier in NO fill in Annexure 1 
Step 10: For  family Identifier in Yes fill in Ration Card 
Step 11: Fill in all Family Details, Personal details, address details, bank details, Choose Cylinder Types, Select rural or urban and select the declaration and submit.
Step 12: Generate the Reference number and Print your applied form attach all the documents you submitted online and visit the selected Gas agency.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be at least 18 years old. 1. The applicant’s household must not have any existing LPG connection. 1. The applicant woman must belong to any one of the eligible categories. - SC Households - ST Households - Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin) - Most Backward Classes - Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) - Tea and Ex-Tea Garden tribes - Forest Dwellers - People residing on Islands and River Islands - SECC Households (AHL TIN) - Poor Household as per 14-point declaration

children.text: The applicant must be at least 18 years old.
The applicant’s household must not have any existing LPG connection.
The applicant woman must belong to any one of the eligible categories.
SC Households
ST Households
Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin)
Most Backward Classes
Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY)
Tea and Ex-Tea Garden tribes
Forest Dwellers
People residing on Islands and River Islands
SECC Households (AHL TIN)
Poor Household as per 14-point declaration
